behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert specific implementation details not mentioned in instruction.md: test_jsg_catch_scope_class_declared checks for 'class JsgCatchScope', 'getCaughtException', and 'catchException' method names. test_jsg_catch_scope_implementation checks for 'JsgCatchScope::catchException' in jsg.c++. The instruction never mentions the class name 'JsgCatchScope' or the method names 'getCaughtException'/'catchException'. Also test_readme_documents_exception_handling_update checks for literal strings 'js

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what to build (macros JSG_TRY/JSG_CATCH, migration of call sites, documentation updates) but does not specify exact implementation code, line numbers, or the specific class/method structure needed. The agent must design the supporting infrastructure.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert gold-specific implementation details: test_jsg_catch_scope_class_declared requires exactly 'class JsgCatchScope', 'getCaughtException', and 'catchException' as identifiers. test_jsg_catch_scope_implementation requires 'JsgCatchScope::catchException' in jsg.c++. An alternative correct implementation could use different class/method names (e.g., 'JsgTryCatchHelper', 'getException', 'handleException') and would fail these tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All fail-to-pass tests are text/grep-based checks: grep for '#define JSG_TRY', grep for 'JsgCatchScope::catchException', string presence checks in files. No test compiles the code, runs it, or checks behavioral correctness. Every test is essentially 'assert string in file_content' or subprocess grep.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub implementation that merely adds '#define JSG_TRY(js)' and '#define JSG_CATCH(exception)' to jsg.h, adds a class declaration with the right method names, puts a stub in jsg.c++, adds JSG_TRY text to migrated files and README would pass all tests without implementing any actual exception handling functionality. No test verifies compilation or runtime behavior.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction requires: automatic conversion of JsExceptionThrown and kj::Exception to jsg::Value, setup of try/catch infrastructure on construction, nested usage support, ExceptionToJsOptions support, if-else friendliness. None of these behavioral requirements are tested - only string presence checks are performed.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining what each test checks.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task produces C++ macros, class implementations, and documentation - not structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: File paths, function names, macro names, and commands appear correct and consistent across instruction.md and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction specifies which files to modify (jsg.h, jsg.c++, README.md, and migration targets) which is appropriate for the task scope. It doesn't leak specific line numbers or exact code changes.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code implementation task, not an agentmd-edits or config-only task.
